title: Reviewing AI Code Is Not A Viable Argument
excerpt: I am a skeptic of the utility of LLM in software development. It is not because of IP laws problems (even if they are highly problematic), nor is it for ecological and ressource comsumptions reasons. It is not even for “they are all crap” reasons. My problem with LLM Coding Assistants is that I cannot see, in the face of the scientific evidence, how they can help someone write code better or faster.
content_html:
I am a skeptic of the utility of LLM in software development. It is not because of IP laws problems (even if they are highly problematic), nor is it for ecological and ressource comsumptions reasons. It is not even for “they are all crap” reasons. My problem with LLM Coding Assistants is that I cannot see, in the face of the scientific evidence, how they can help someone write code better or faster.

And the thing that irritate me is that it seems that none of the proponents of LLM Coding Assistants seems to ever address this problem and this evidence when they defend their tooling choices. Worse, it seems that they give fuel to my arguments everytime they write a rebuke of the skeptics. So let’s look at what I have a problem with, how empirical scientific research support that view, how proponents of LLM Coding Assistants could show this is not a problem and then how right now they are doing the actual opposite.

Note: This piece was written nearly a year ago, hence why you may find vocabulary, like “Coding Assistants” that has been mostly replaced at this point. Sadly, I have not been able to find a term in the current vocabulary around genAI used to code that covers all the use case. So I kept the “Coding Assistants” wording.

The Intern Problem

The fundamental problem of LLM Coding Assistants that my criticism center on is their relatively high risk of getting things wrong. For all kind of reasons, some structural to how LLM works and other more akin to the interfaces we provide to interact with them, LLM Coding Assistants get things wrong. It can be hallucinations, typos, simply doing something that is not linked to the task demanded, going into a different path, etc etc.

A lot of people I have talked about that experimented with LLM Coding Assistants explains that they feel “Like an intern”. Like an intern, you should not expect too much from them, you should expect that everything they do will be more or less wrong, and that they have no idea what they are doing, but are highly enthusiastic. I see they never got me as an intern. I was definitely not enthusiastic.

And their answer to that problem, the one you will see all over the internet, is simple. You just do the same thing that you do with interns and junior developers in your team. No, they do not mean you put everything they did in the bin and forget about it. What they mean is that you should review all the code yourself. I mean, you are the human that know better. And you are the one responsible for the code anyway. And on top of this, you are doing that for all the code that get into your codebase anyway, you do not let code get in without a review, right?

What We Means When We Say Reviews

First of all, I want to be clear here. There are different practices in the litterature and in the profession grouped under the term “review”. So let’s be explicit here. Seeing the degree of (mis)trust and potential mistakes there, we should not accept the kind of “lightweight and heavily distributed” reviews that we see the most in our industry as the standard for LLM Coding Assistants supervision by a professional developer. They are not a bad thing to do, nor are they inefficient, but they have been shown in the litterature mostly as good to distribute knowledge of changes and as a way to enforce all kind of surface level rules.

For AI Coding Asssistants, we will need a proper “code review”. Not something as formal and complex as reviews of old, by committees, painstakingly checking every line one by one over a few hours. But still, we want something quite involved and complete. After all, these are interns writing sometimes highly complex code. And if there is something we know in software, it is that the devil can be in the details.

The Limits Of Reviews

Without going into some philosophical depth of reviewing as a practice, there is a glaring problem in this idea. From all the research we have, we have learned, empirically a few things about code reviews. And the evidence is relatively solid here, within reasonable limits. You will see that these do not matter there.

  1. A review that last more than 1h is too long.
  2. A review that has to be effective cannot be more than 400LOC at a time, in that time.

Empirical research has shown that reviews that are longer than 1h quickly reach diminishing returns whatever is the size of the code being reviewed. So this is not only that people cannot find bugs anymore after 1h because they already thoroughly reviewed most of the code. No, it is more linked to the fact that after 1h at that level of attention, people start getting tired, bored and simply need some time off.

Of note is the total absence of research as far as I could find, on the recovery time needed between review sessions of 1h. So I canot tell you how frequently someone could do 1h review sessions. But we could probably accept an extreme maximum limit of a handful per day. Which is probably far more than most people could do, I would probably put the average at 2, but eh. That is still in the right ballpark.

The second limit that has been seen in empirical research is that speed, that is number of Lines Of Code per Hour (LOC/H) is highly variable, mostly depending on the context of the code, the kind of code being reviewed, experience, knowledge and the rest of your expected reasons. But something that is regularly pointed out is that, even if there is no hard cut off, it seems that a maximum of 400 LOC/H is a good maximum speed acceptable for efficience, as nearly no review above this speed seems particularly effective in the empirical data at finding and flagging defects.

What It Means For LLMs

So, if we combine the claim that the solution to LLM Coding Assistants problems is to review the code, with the empirical evidence from scientific research on code reviews, what do we get? For every 400 LOC written by a LLM Coding Assistants (at best, less for code that are hard to review), we need one professional senior developer to spend 1h of his time reviewing the code. And he only has between 10 to 40 of these reviewing slots per week, with a recovery period in between of unknown lenght, but probably of at least an hour or two.

And that is without taking into account that these slots of intense concentration are probably in high demand. Meetings, code to think through, design sessions, incidents, etc. You better hope you can take all of them. This means that in the best case scenario, a developer using a LLM Coding Assistant can write, review and commit, at best, a few thousands LOC per day to a codebase. And that is the best case scenario, a more realistic one is less than 1k LOC per day.

If that sound high, remember that this comprise everything. Boilerplate, tests, migrations, configuration, etc. And that this is the best case scenario, where most of the code is boilerplate and easy to review. I have basic unique test files that are more than 400 LOC. So the productivity of said developer is not going to be really high. Hell, if anything, this sounds like a real ceiling to how good these LLM Coding Assistants can help in writing code.

But Wait, It Gets Worse

This makes the “It is ok, you just have to review everything like usual” argument complex to believe in. After all, even if they were right, it would not really provide a speed up or that much more productivity, due to the limits of code reviews. On top of this, remember that this is empirical evidence coming from research on how human reviewers find human coders defects in code. We have no evidence that humans reviewing LLM Coding Assistants code are as efficient as code review by humans of humans code. If anything, we have some tentative evidence that humans that review code generated by LLM Coding Assistants are more confident that they found all the defects, while finding less defects.

The output of Human Reviewer teamed with a LLM Coding Assistants is of lesser quality than the one of Human Reviewer teamed with a Human Coder. But the reviewer in the first case believe that they did a better job. So not only is the “just review everything” practice leading to high limits on productivity benefit of using the LLM Coding Assistants, we do not even have solid proof that it even works at all to fix the problem of LLM Coding Assistants being wrong, a lot.

Note that I never talk of the cost of fixing the defects here. I am only talking of the ability of professional developers, in a professional environment, of reviewing code in order to flag problems. This is not even about how costly the defects introduced by LLM Coding Assistants are. This is about how costly reviewing the code generated is. Whatever the defects are or how many of them there are. Even if the LLM Coding Assistants were extremely good, these costs would stay the same and the productivity hit the same limits.

I Mean, It Could Be Worse

Well. Bad News. It does get worse. There is a benefit of using LLM Coding Assistants regularly touted by developers that are proponents of them and are going all in in using them as part of their professional tools. It is that they can type and produce the code that is the most painful to author and deal with in your professional life. Here is a quote from a proeminent and visible “bash the skeptic” blogpost contemporary to me writing this, talking of what LLM Coding Assistants can produce.

Also: 100% of all the Bash code you should author ever again

I want to stop you there for a minute, so you can think through what the author mean here. He means that you do not have to write your shellscripts yourself. You can now let the computer, the LLM Coding Assistant, do that torturous painful part. No need to lose you sanity anymore, you can let the code that is the easiest to get wrong, the harder to review, the harder to understand you made a fatal mistake that will blow up everything be written and handled by the machine. The code that is so loose on parsing but also so semantically overloaded that a simple typo in a ponctuation could be utterly harmless or literally make you delete a whole computer. That code.

Well you can just let the thing that get everything wrong randomly all the time write it. And then you can just review it and never have to write it ever again. I mean what could go wrong. This is not like it is the hardest code to review for mistakes right? Right? RIGHT !? I mean, maybe they have not at all addressed the question of if we actually can review LLM Coding Assistant output in a way that works out.

But for sure, they are not touting as the main use of this the code that is also the hardest to review effectively. RIGHT ?! Yeah they are. And this is where they lost me. They do not stop at not engaging with the argument of “are you sure that reviewing the code solve the problems?” or the “but if we do all that reviewing,is it really more productive and better?”. No they go deeper. They offer the code that we know, as a professional field, is the hardest to review and get right, as the best application for LLM Coding Assistants.

What To Do To Make Me Calm Down

Ok, I will calm down. Sure, noone engaged with my argument, sure I keep pointing at problems, sure I yell wolf at people using these tools to make their life easier. All nice, keep yelling at cloud, but how could someone convince me? After all, I could be wrong, can’t I? I mean, if I use science all the time to support my point, I should also have some ideas of what could prove me wrong, no?

Yes. First of all, I would like to see empirical research on the ability of humans reviewers to find defect in LLM generated code. And how fast. And how much we can do it per day. We already have empirical study for this in reviewing code generated by humans, so if you want to convince me my argument is wrong, we will need a set of data that is human reviewing LLMs. We have some experimental data and some empirical data already, but the dataset is limited in size and in context. More reproduction would help. Note however that so far, the scientific data point that human are bad at reviewing LLM (or that LLM are good at avoiding detection). This is consistent with expectations, LLM are trained to evade detection. But maybe this was a fluke.

Secondly, you could try to show us how the review of LLM generated content is different from reviewing human generated content. Maybe all this data and empirical evidence we have is not applicable here. Maybe LLMs are sufficiently different that this is a qualitatively different problem. In which case, my arguments against LLM Coding Assistant fall apart. Note, once again, that so far there is tentative burgeoning evidence that reviewing LLM generated content is, indeed, different. But it is point at reviewing it being harder than human generated content, which would make the argument I present here even more powerful. So beware of what knowledge you search.

And Yet I Am Mad

The argument above is not the only reason I am an AI Skeptic, but it is probably the biggest one I have against LLMs Coding Assistants. Mostly, I cannot see, knowing all of this information about code reviews, how we can get benefit, as professional developers, out of LLM tools using this interface and process. It flies against everything we know about producing quality code.

But the thing that really drives me crazy is not that we keep getting these tools and process offered again and again by vendors, despite said scientific evidence. No what drive me crazy and angry is being called “nuts” by someone that never even engage with the problem or the question I asked. I have yet to see a single one of the AI proponent even engage with the problem or try to do empirical evidence research on this.

I am used to this behaviour. I have seen the same thing play again and again in our field around things like TDD, types systems, separating test and coding teams, CI/CD, devops, etc. Anecdotal data always win in our field, even if it flies against everything the empirical evidence shows. But still. I would like if our field stopped at bloodletting. Let’s try to have an actual discussion about ergonomics and empirical evidence. If you want to convince me, please stop calling me nuts or bringing anecdotal information about “hey this time it worked for me”. Start looking at how we got all this empirical evidence around code review in the first place and go do some studies. Please. Let’s use that moment to start taking our professional tools seriously.

It can be hallucinations, typos, simply doing something that is not linked to the task demanded, going into a different path, etc etc.

sentences.en: The fundamental problem of LLM Coding Assistants that my criticism center on is their relatively high risk of getting things wrong.
sentences.zh: 我批评的LLM编码助理的根本问题是他们出错的风险相对较高。

sentences.en: For all kind of reasons, some structural to how LLM works and other more akin to the interfaces we provide to interact with them, LLM Coding Assistants get things wrong.
sentences.zh: 由于各种原因，一些与 LLM 工作方式有关的结构原因以及其他更类似于我们提供的与其交互的界面，LLM 编码助理会出错。

sentences.en: It can be hallucinations, typos, simply doing something that is not linked to the task demanded, going into a different path, etc etc.
sentences.zh: 它可能是幻觉、打字错误、只是做了一些与所要求的任务无关的事情、进入了不同的路径等等。

blocks.html_en: A lot of people I have talked about that experimented with LLM Coding Assistants explains that they feel “Like an intern”. Like an intern, you should not expect too much from them, you should expect that everything they do will be more or less wrong, and that they have no idea what they are doing, but are highly enthusiastic. I see they never got me as an intern. I was definitely not enthusiastic.

sentences.en: A lot of people I have talked about that experimented with LLM Coding Assistants explains that they feel “Like an intern”.
sentences.zh: 我谈过的很多尝试过 LLM 编码助理的人都解释说，他们感觉“就像一名实习生”。

sentences.en: Like an intern, you should not expect too much from them, you should expect that everything they do will be more or less wrong, and that they have no idea what they are doing, but are highly enthusiastic.
sentences.zh: 就像实习生一样，你不应该对他们期望太高，你应该预料到他们所做的一切或多或少都会有错误，而且他们不知道自己在做什么，但热情很高。

sentences.en: I see they never got me as an intern.
sentences.zh: 我发现他们从来没有让我当实习生。

sentences.en: I was definitely not enthusiastic.
sentences.zh: 我绝对不热情。

blocks.html_en: And their answer to that problem, the one you will see all over the internet, is simple. You just do the same thing that you do with interns and junior developers in your team. No, they do not mean you put everything they did in the bin and forget about it. What they mean is that you should review all the code yourself. I mean, you are the human that know better. And you are the one responsible for the code anyway. And on top of this, you are doing that for all the code that get into your codebase anyway, you do not let code get in without a review, right?

sentences.en: And their answer to that problem, the one you will see all over the internet, is simple.
sentences.zh: 他们对这个问题的回答（你在互联网上随处可见）很简单。

sentences.en: You just do the same thing that you do with interns and junior developers in your team.
sentences.zh: 您只需做与团队中的实习生和初级开发人员相同的事情即可。

sentences.en: No, they do not mean you put everything they did in the bin and forget about it.
sentences.zh: 不，他们并不意味着你把他们所做的一切都扔进垃圾箱然后忘记。

sentences.en: What they mean is that you should review all the code yourself.
sentences.zh: 他们的意思是您应该自己检查所有代码。

sentences.en: I mean, you are the human that know better.
sentences.zh: 我的意思是，你是最了解情况的人。

sentences.en: And you are the one responsible for the code anyway.
sentences.zh: 无论如何，你是对代码负责的人。

sentences.en: And on top of this, you are doing that for all the code that get into your codebase anyway, you do not let code get in without a review, right?
sentences.zh: 最重要的是，无论如何，您都会对进入代码库的所有代码执行此操作，您不会让代码在未经审查的情况下进入，对吗？

blocks.html_en: What We Means When We Say Reviews

sentences.en: What We Means When We Say Reviews
sentences.zh: 当我们说评论时我们的意思是什么

blocks.html_en: First of all, I want to be clear here. There are different practices in the litterature and in the profession grouped under the term “review”. So let’s be explicit here. Seeing the degree of (mis)trust and potential mistakes there, we should not accept the kind of “lightweight and heavily distributed” reviews that we see the most in our industry as the standard for LLM Coding Assistants supervision by a professional developer. They are not a bad thing to do, nor are they inefficient, but they have been shown in the litterature mostly as good to distribute knowledge of changes and as a way to enforce all kind of surface level rules.

sentences.en: First of all, I want to be clear here.
sentences.zh: 首先，我想在这里澄清一下。

sentences.en: There are different practices in the litterature and in the profession grouped under the term “review”.
sentences.zh: 文献和专业中有不同的实践被归为“审查”一词。

sentences.en: So let’s be explicit here.
sentences.zh: 所以我们在这里明确一下。

sentences.en: Seeing the degree of (mis)trust and potential mistakes there, we should not accept the kind of “lightweight and heavily distributed” reviews that we see the most in our industry as the standard for LLM Coding Assistants supervision by a professional developer.
sentences.zh: 考虑到那里的（不）信任程度和潜在错误，我们不应该接受我们行业中最常见的那种“轻量级和高度分布”的评论，作为专业开发人员监督 LLM 编码助理的标准。

sentences.en: They are not a bad thing to do, nor are they inefficient, but they have been shown in the litterature mostly as good to distribute knowledge of changes and as a way to enforce all kind of surface level rules.
sentences.zh: 它们并不是一件坏事，也不是效率低下，但文献表明它们大多可以很好地分发变化知识，并作为执行各种表面规则的一种方式。

blocks.html_en: For AI Coding Asssistants, we will need a proper “code review”. Not something as formal and complex as reviews of old, by committees, painstakingly checking every line one by one over a few hours. But still, we want something quite involved and complete. After all, these are interns writing sometimes highly complex code. And if there is something we know in software, it is that the devil can be in the details.

sentences.en: For AI Coding Asssistants, we will need a proper “code review”.
sentences.zh: 对于人工智能编码助手，我们需要适当的“代码审查”。

sentences.en: Not something as formal and complex as reviews of old, by committees, painstakingly checking every line one by one over a few hours.
sentences.zh: 不像旧的审查那样正式和复杂，由委员会在几个小时内煞费苦心地逐条检查每一行。

sentences.en: But still, we want something quite involved and complete.
sentences.zh: 但我们仍然想要一些相当复杂和完整的东西。

sentences.en: After all, these are interns writing sometimes highly complex code.
sentences.zh: 毕竟，这些实习生有时会编写高度复杂的代码。

sentences.en: And if there is something we know in software, it is that the devil can be in the details.
sentences.zh: 如果说软件中有什么是我们所知道的，那就是细节决定成败。

blocks.html_en: The Limits Of Reviews

sentences.en: The Limits Of Reviews
sentences.zh: 评论的局限性

blocks.html_en: Without going into some philosophical depth of reviewing as a practice, there is a glaring problem in this idea. From all the research we have, we have learned, empirically a few things about code reviews. And the evidence is relatively solid here, within reasonable limits. You will see that these do not matter there.

sentences.en: Without going into some philosophical depth of reviewing as a practice, there is a glaring problem in this idea.
sentences.zh: 如果不深入探讨作为实践的复习的哲学深度，这个想法就存在一个明显的问题。

sentences.en: From all the research we have, we have learned, empirically a few things about code reviews.
sentences.zh: 从我们所做的所有研究中，我们凭经验了解到了一些关于代码审查的事情。

sentences.en: And the evidence is relatively solid here, within reasonable limits.
sentences.zh: 这里的证据相对可靠，在合理范围内。

sentences.en: You will see that these do not matter there.
sentences.zh: 你会发现这些在那里并不重要。

blocks.html_en: • A review that last more than 1h is too long.

sentences.en: A review that last more than 1h is too long.
sentences.zh: 持续超过1小时的评论太长了。

blocks.html_en: • A review that has to be effective cannot be more than 400LOC at a time, in that time.

sentences.en: A review that has to be effective cannot be more than 400LOC at a time, in that time.
sentences.zh: 一次必须有效的审核不能超过 400LOC。

blocks.html_en: Empirical research has shown that reviews that are longer than 1h quickly reach diminishing returns whatever is the size of the code being reviewed. So this is not only that people cannot find bugs anymore after 1h because they already thoroughly reviewed most of the code. No, it is more linked to the fact that after 1h at that level of attention, people start getting tired, bored and simply need some time off.

sentences.en: Empirical research has shown that reviews that are longer than 1h quickly reach diminishing returns whatever is the size of the code being reviewed.
sentences.zh: 实证研究表明，无论被审查的代码有多大，超过 1 小时的审查很快就会达到收益递减的效果。

sentences.en: So this is not only that people cannot find bugs anymore after 1h because they already thoroughly reviewed most of the code.
sentences.zh: 所以这不仅仅是人们在 1 小时后无法再发现 bug，因为他们已经彻底审查了大部分代码。

sentences.en: No, it is more linked to the fact that after 1h at that level of attention, people start getting tired, bored and simply need some time off.
sentences.zh: 不，这更与这样一个事实有关：在这种注意力水平1小时后，人们开始感到疲倦、无聊，只是需要休息一下。

blocks.html_en: Of note is the total absence of research as far as I could find, on the recovery time needed between review sessions of 1h. So I canot tell you how frequently someone could do 1h review sessions. But we could probably accept an extreme maximum limit of a handful per day. Which is probably far more than most people could do, I would probably put the average at 2, but eh. That is still in the right ballpark.

sentences.en: Of note is the total absence of research as far as I could find, on the recovery time needed between review sessions of 1h.
sentences.zh: 值得注意的是，据我所知，完全没有关于 1 小时复习课程之间所需恢复时间的研究。

sentences.en: So I canot tell you how frequently someone could do 1h review sessions.
sentences.zh: 所以我无法告诉你多久有人可以进行 1 小时的复习会议。

sentences.en: But we could probably accept an extreme maximum limit of a handful per day.
sentences.zh: 但我们可能可以接受每天一把的极端最大限制。

sentences.en: Which is probably far more than most people could do, I would probably put the average at 2, but eh.
sentences.zh: 这可能远远超过大多数人能做到的，我可能会把平均值定为 2，但是呃。

sentences.en: That is still in the right ballpark.
sentences.zh: 这仍然在正确的范围内。

blocks.html_en: The second limit that has been seen in empirical research is that speed, that is number of Lines Of Code per Hour (LOC/H) is highly variable, mostly depending on the context of the code, the kind of code being reviewed, experience, knowledge and the rest of your expected reasons. But something that is regularly pointed out is that, even if there is no hard cut off, it seems that a maximum of 400 LOC/H is a good maximum speed acceptable for efficience, as nearly no review above this speed seems particularly effective in the empirical data at finding and flagging defects.

sentences.en: The second limit that has been seen in empirical research is that speed, that is number of Lines Of Code per Hour (LOC/H) is highly variable, mostly depending on the context of the code, the kind of code being reviewed, experience, knowledge and the rest of your expected reasons.
sentences.zh: 实证研究中发现的第二个限制是速度，即每小时的代码行数 (LOC/H) 变化很大，主要取决于代码的上下文、正在审查的代码类型、经验、知识以及其他预期原因。

sentences.en: But something that is regularly pointed out is that, even if there is no hard cut off, it seems that a maximum of 400 LOC/H is a good maximum speed acceptable for efficience, as nearly no review above this speed seems particularly effective in the empirical data at finding and flagging defects.
sentences.zh: 但经常指出的一点是，即使没有硬切断，最大 400 LOC/H 似乎也是效率可接受的良好最大速度，因为在经验数据中几乎没有高于此速度的审查在发现和标记缺陷方面特别有效。

blocks.html_en: What It Means For LLMs

sentences.en: What It Means For LLMs
sentences.zh: 这对法学硕士意味着什么

blocks.html_en: So, if we combine the claim that the solution to LLM Coding Assistants problems is to review the code, with the empirical evidence from scientific research on code reviews, what do we get? For every 400 LOC written by a LLM Coding Assistants (at best, less for code that are hard to review), we need one professional senior developer to spend 1h of his time reviewing the code. And he only has between 10 to 40 of these reviewing slots per week, with a recovery period in between of unknown lenght, but probably of at least an hour or two.

sentences.en: So, if we combine the claim that the solution to LLM Coding Assistants problems is to review the code, with the empirical evidence from scientific research on code reviews, what do we get?
sentences.zh: 那么，如果我们将“LLM编码助理问题的解决方案是审查代码”这一说法与代码审查科学研究的经验证据结合起来，我们会得到什么呢？

sentences.en: For every 400 LOC written by a LLM Coding Assistants (at best, less for code that are hard to review), we need one professional senior developer to spend 1h of his time reviewing the code.
sentences.zh: 对于LLM编码助理编写的每400个LOC（最多，对于难以审查的代码来说更少），我们需要一名专业的高级开发人员花费1个小时的时间来审查代码。

sentences.en: And he only has between 10 to 40 of these reviewing slots per week, with a recovery period in between of unknown lenght, but probably of at least an hour or two.
sentences.zh: 而且他每周只有 10 到 40 个这样的复习时间，恢复期的长度未知，但可能至少有一两个小时。

blocks.html_en: And that is without taking into account that these slots of intense concentration are probably in high demand. Meetings, code to think through, design sessions, incidents, etc. You better hope you can take all of them. This means that in the best case scenario, a developer using a LLM Coding Assistant can write, review and commit, at best, a few thousands LOC per day to a codebase. And that is the best case scenario, a more realistic one is less than 1k LOC per day.

sentences.en: And that is without taking into account that these slots of intense concentration are probably in high demand.
sentences.zh: 这还没有考虑到这些高度集中的时段可能需求量很大。

sentences.en: Meetings, code to think through, design sessions, incidents, etc. You better hope you can take all of them.
sentences.zh: 会议、需要思考的代码、设计会议、事件等等。你最好希望你能全部参加。

sentences.en: This means that in the best case scenario, a developer using a LLM Coding Assistant can write, review and commit, at best, a few thousands LOC per day to a codebase.
sentences.zh: 这意味着在最好的情况下，使用 LLM 编码助手的开发人员每天最多可以编写、审查和提交数千个 LOC 到代码库。

sentences.en: And that is the best case scenario, a more realistic one is less than 1k LOC per day.
sentences.zh: 这是最好的情况，更现实的情况是每天少于 1k LOC。

blocks.html_en: If that sound high, remember that this comprise everything. Boilerplate, tests, migrations, configuration, etc. And that this is the best case scenario, where most of the code is boilerplate and easy to review. I have basic unique test files that are more than 400 LOC. So the productivity of said developer is not going to be really high. Hell, if anything, this sounds like a real ceiling to how good these LLM Coding Assistants can help in writing code.

sentences.en: If that sound high, remember that this comprise everything.
sentences.zh: 如果这听起来很高，请记住这包含了一切。

sentences.en: Boilerplate, tests, migrations, configuration, etc. And that this is the best case scenario, where most of the code is boilerplate and easy to review.
sentences.zh: 样板文件、测试、迁移、配置等。这是最好的情况，其中大部分代码都是样板文件并且易于审查。

sentences.en: I have basic unique test files that are more than 400 LOC.
sentences.zh: 我有超过 400 个 LOC 的基本独特测试文件。

sentences.en: So the productivity of said developer is not going to be really high.
sentences.zh: 因此，上述开发人员的生产力不会很高。

sentences.en: Hell, if anything, this sounds like a real ceiling to how good these LLM Coding Assistants can help in writing code.
sentences.zh: 地狱，如果有的话，这听起来像是这些法学硕士编码助理可以帮助编写代码的真正上限。

blocks.html_en: But Wait, It Gets Worse

sentences.en: But Wait, It Gets Worse
sentences.zh: 但等等，情况会变得更糟

blocks.html_en: This makes the “It is ok, you just have to review everything like usual” argument complex to believe in. After all, even if they were right, it would not really provide a speed up or that much more productivity, due to the limits of code reviews. On top of this, remember that this is empirical evidence coming from research on how human reviewers find human coders defects in code. We have no evidence that humans reviewing LLM Coding Assistants code are as efficient as code review by humans of humans code. If anything, we have some tentative evidence that humans that review code generated by LLM Coding Assistants are more confident that they found all the defects, while finding less defects.

sentences.en: This makes the “It is ok, you just have to review everything like usual” argument complex to believe in.
sentences.zh: 这使得“没关系，你只需像往常一样回顾一切”的论点变得复杂而难以相信。

sentences.en: After all, even if they were right, it would not really provide a speed up or that much more productivity, due to the limits of code reviews.
sentences.zh: 毕竟，即使他们是对的，由于代码审查的限制，它也不会真正提供加速或更高的生产力。

sentences.en: On top of this, remember that this is empirical evidence coming from research on how human reviewers find human coders defects in code.
sentences.zh: 最重要的是，请记住，这是来自对人类审阅者如何发现人类编码员代码缺陷的研究的经验证据。

sentences.en: We have no evidence that humans reviewing LLM Coding Assistants code are as efficient as code review by humans of humans code.
sentences.zh: 我们没有证据表明人类审查 LLM 编码助理代码的效率与人类代码审查的效率相同。

sentences.en: If anything, we have some tentative evidence that humans that review code generated by LLM Coding Assistants are more confident that they found all the defects, while finding less defects.
sentences.zh: 如果有的话，我们有一些初步证据表明，审查 LLM 编码助理生成的代码的人更有信心他们发现了所有缺陷，同时发现了更少的缺陷。

blocks.html_en: The output of Human Reviewer teamed with a LLM Coding Assistants is of lesser quality than the one of Human Reviewer teamed with a Human Coder. But the reviewer in the first case believe that they did a better job. So not only is the “just review everything” practice leading to high limits on productivity benefit of using the LLM Coding Assistants, we do not even have solid proof that it even works at all to fix the problem of LLM Coding Assistants being wrong, a lot.

sentences.en: The output of Human Reviewer teamed with a LLM Coding Assistants is of lesser quality than the one of Human Reviewer teamed with a Human Coder.
sentences.zh: 人类审阅者与法学硕士编码助理合作的输出质量低于人类审阅者与人类编码员合作的输出质量。

sentences.en: But the reviewer in the first case believe that they did a better job.
sentences.zh: 但第一个案例的审稿人认为他们做得更好。

sentences.en: So not only is the “just review everything” practice leading to high limits on productivity benefit of using the LLM Coding Assistants, we do not even have solid proof that it even works at all to fix the problem of LLM Coding Assistants being wrong, a lot.
sentences.zh: 因此，“只审查所有内容”的做法不仅会导致使用 LLM 编码助手的生产力效益受到很大限制，而且我们甚至没有确凿的证据证明它根本可以解决 LLM 编码助手错误很多的问题。

blocks.html_en: Note that I never talk of the cost of fixing the defects here. I am only talking of the ability of professional developers, in a professional environment, of reviewing code in order to flag problems. This is not even about how costly the defects introduced by LLM Coding Assistants are. This is about how costly reviewing the code generated is. Whatever the defects are or how many of them there are. Even if the LLM Coding Assistants were extremely good, these costs would stay the same and the productivity hit the same limits.

sentences.en: Note that I never talk of the cost of fixing the defects here.
sentences.zh: 请注意，我在这里从未谈论修复缺陷的成本。

sentences.en: I am only talking of the ability of professional developers, in a professional environment, of reviewing code in order to flag problems.
sentences.zh: 我只是谈论专业开发人员在专业环境中审查代码以标记问题的能力。

sentences.en: This is not even about how costly the defects introduced by LLM Coding Assistants are.
sentences.zh: 这甚至还没有涉及 LLM 编码助理带来的缺陷的代价有多大。

sentences.en: This is about how costly reviewing the code generated is.
sentences.zh: 这是关于审查生成的代码的成本有多大。

sentences.en: Whatever the defects are or how many of them there are.
sentences.zh: 无论缺陷是什么或有多少缺陷。

sentences.en: Even if the LLM Coding Assistants were extremely good, these costs would stay the same and the productivity hit the same limits.
sentences.zh: 即使法学硕士编码助理非常优秀，这些成本也将保持不变，生产力也会达到相同的极限。

blocks.html_en: I Mean, It Could Be Worse

sentences.en: I Mean, It Could Be Worse
sentences.zh: 我的意思是，情况可能会更糟

blocks.html_en: Well. Bad News. It does get worse. There is a benefit of using LLM Coding Assistants regularly touted by developers that are proponents of them and are going all in in using them as part of their professional tools. It is that they can type and produce the code that is the most painful to author and deal with in your professional life. Here is a quote from a proeminent and visible “bash the skeptic” blogpost contemporary to me writing this, talking of what LLM Coding Assistants can produce.

sentences.en: Well.
sentences.zh: 出色地。

sentences.en: Bad News.
sentences.zh: 坏消息。

sentences.en: It does get worse.
sentences.zh: 情况确实变得更糟。

sentences.en: There is a benefit of using LLM Coding Assistants regularly touted by developers that are proponents of them and are going all in in using them as part of their professional tools.
sentences.zh: 使用 LLM 编码助手有一个好处，开发人员经常吹捧它们，并全力以赴将它们用作专业工具的一部分。

sentences.en: It is that they can type and produce the code that is the most painful to author and deal with in your professional life.
sentences.zh: 他们可以输入和生成职业生涯中编写和处理最痛苦的代码。

sentences.en: Here is a quote from a proeminent and visible “bash the skeptic” blogpost contemporary to me writing this, talking of what LLM Coding Assistants can produce.
sentences.zh: 这里引用了我写这篇文章时的一篇著名的、引人注目的“抨击怀疑论者”博客文章，谈论了法学硕士编码助理可以产生什么。

blocks.html_en: Also: 100% of all the Bash code you should author ever again

sentences.en: Also: 100% of all the Bash code you should author ever again
sentences.zh: 另外：您应该再次编写 100% 的 Bash 代码

blocks.html_en: Also: 100% of all the Bash code you should author ever again

sentences.en: Also: 100% of all the Bash code you should author ever again
sentences.zh: 另外：您应该再次编写 100% 的 Bash 代码

blocks.html_en: I want to stop you there for a minute, so you can think through what the author mean here. He means that you do not have to write your shellscripts yourself. You can now let the computer, the LLM Coding Assistant, do that torturous painful part. No need to lose you sanity anymore, you can let the code that is the easiest to get wrong, the harder to review, the harder to understand you made a fatal mistake that will blow up everything be written and handled by the machine. The code that is so loose on parsing but also so semantically overloaded that a simple typo in a ponctuation could be utterly harmless or literally make you delete a whole computer. That code.

content_html_zh:
我对 LLM 在软件开发中的实用性持怀疑态度。 这不是因为知识产权法问题（即使它们问题很大），也不是因为生态和资源消耗的原因。 这甚至不是出于“他们都是垃圾”的原因。 我对法学硕士编码助理的问题是，面对科学证据，我看不到他们如何帮助某人更好或更快地编写代码。

让我恼火的是，似乎没有一个 LLM 编码助理的支持者在捍卫自己的工具选择时解决过这个问题和证据。 更糟糕的是，每当他们写下对怀疑论者的谴责时，他们似乎都在为我的论点提供燃料。 因此，让我们看看我的问题是什么，实证科学研究如何支持这一观点，LLM 编码助理的支持者如何证明这不是问题，然后他们现在如何做相反的事情。

注意：这篇文章是近一年前写的，因此您可能会发现诸如“编码助手”之类的词汇此时大部分已被替换。 遗憾的是，我无法在当前用于编码的 genAI 词汇中找到涵盖所有用例的术语。 所以我保留了“编码助理”的措辞。

实习生问题

我批评的LLM编码助理的根本问题是他们出错的风险相对较高。 由于各种原因，一些与 LLM 工作方式有关的结构原因以及其他更类似于我们提供的与其交互的界面，LLM 编码助理会出错。 它可能是幻觉、打字错误、只是做了一些与所要求的任务无关的事情、进入了不同的路径等等。

我谈过的很多尝试过 LLM 编码助理的人都解释说，他们感觉“就像一名实习生”。 就像实习生一样，你不应该对他们期望太高，你应该预料到他们所做的一切或多或少都会有错误，而且他们不知道自己在做什么，但热情很高。 我发现他们从来没有让我当实习生。 我绝对不热情。

他们对这个问题的回答（你在互联网上随处可见）很简单。 您只需做与团队中的实习生和初级开发人员相同的事情即可。 不，他们并不意味着你把他们所做的一切都扔进垃圾箱然后忘记。 他们的意思是您应该自己检查所有代码。 我的意思是，你是最了解情况的人。 无论如何，你是对代码负责的人。 最重要的是，无论如何，您都会对进入代码库的所有代码执行此操作，您不会让代码在未经审查的情况下进入，对吗？

当我们说评论时我们的意思是什么

首先，我想在这里澄清一下。 文献和专业中有不同的实践被归为“审查”一词。 所以我们在这里明确一下。 考虑到那里的（不）信任程度和潜在错误，我们不应该接受我们行业中最常见的那种“轻量级和高度分布”的评论，作为专业开发人员监督 LLM 编码助理的标准。 它们并不是一件坏事，也不是效率低下，但文献表明它们大多可以很好地分发变化知识，并作为执行各种表面规则的一种方式。

对于人工智能编码助手，我们需要适当的“代码审查”。 不像旧的审查那样正式和复杂，由委员会在几个小时内煞费苦心地逐条检查每一行。 但我们仍然想要一些相当复杂和完整的东西。 毕竟，这些实习生有时会编写高度复杂的代码。 如果说软件中有什么是我们所知道的，那就是细节决定成败。

评论的局限性

如果不深入探讨作为实践的复习的哲学深度，这个想法就存在一个明显的问题。 从我们所做的所有研究中，我们凭经验了解到了一些关于代码审查的事情。 这里的证据相对可靠，在合理范围内。 你会发现这些在那里并不重要。

  1. 持续超过1小时的评论太长了。
  2. 一次必须有效的审核不能超过 400LOC。

实证研究表明，无论被审查的代码有多大，超过 1 小时的审查很快就会达到收益递减的效果。 所以这不仅仅是人们在 1 小时后无法再发现 bug，因为他们已经彻底审查了大部分代码。 不，这更与这样一个事实有关：在这种注意力水平1小时后，人们开始感到疲倦、无聊，只是需要休息一下。

值得注意的是，据我所知，完全没有关于 1 小时复习课程之间所需恢复时间的研究。 所以我无法告诉你多久有人可以进行 1 小时的复习会议。 但我们可能可以接受每天一把的极端最大限制。 这可能远远超过大多数人能做到的，我可能会把平均值定为 2，但是呃。 这仍然在正确的范围内。

实证研究中发现的第二个限制是速度，即每小时的代码行数 (LOC/H) 变化很大，主要取决于代码的上下文、正在审查的代码类型、经验、知识以及其他预期原因。 但经常指出的一点是，即使没有硬切断，最大 400 LOC/H 似乎也是效率可接受的良好最大速度，因为在经验数据中几乎没有高于此速度的审查在发现和标记缺陷方面特别有效。

这对法学硕士意味着什么

那么，如果我们将“LLM编码助理问题的解决方案是审查代码”这一说法与代码审查科学研究的经验证据结合起来，我们会得到什么呢？ 对于LLM编码助理编写的每400个LOC（最多，对于难以审查的代码来说更少），我们需要一名专业的高级开发人员花费1个小时的时间来审查代码。 而且他每周只有 10 到 40 个这样的复习时间，恢复期的长度未知，但可能至少有一两个小时。

这还没有考虑到这些高度集中的时段可能需求量很大。 会议、需要思考的代码、设计会议、事件等等。你最好希望你能全部参加。 这意味着在最好的情况下，使用 LLM 编码助手的开发人员每天最多可以编写、审查和提交数千个 LOC 到代码库。 这是最好的情况，更现实的情况是每天少于 1k LOC。

如果这听起来很高，请记住这包含了一切。 样板文件、测试、迁移、配置等。这是最好的情况，其中大部分代码都是样板文件并且易于审查。 我有超过 400 个 LOC 的基本独特测试文件。 因此，上述开发人员的生产力不会很高。 地狱，如果有的话，这听起来像是这些法学硕士编码助理可以帮助编写代码的真正上限。

但等等，情况会变得更糟

这使得“没关系，你只需像往常一样回顾一切”的论点变得复杂而难以相信。 毕竟，即使他们是对的，由于代码审查的限制，它也不会真正提供加速或更高的生产力。 最重要的是，请记住，这是来自对人类审阅者如何发现人类编码员代码缺陷的研究的经验证据。 我们没有证据表明人类审查 LLM 编码助理代码的效率与人类代码审查的效率相同。 如果有的话，我们有一些初步证据表明，审查 LLM 编码助理生成的代码的人更有信心他们发现了所有缺陷，同时发现了更少的缺陷。

人类审阅者与法学硕士编码助理合作的输出质量低于人类审阅者与人类编码员合作的输出质量。 但第一个案例的审稿人认为他们做得更好。 因此，“只审查所有内容”的做法不仅会导致使用 LLM 编码助手的生产力效益受到很大限制，而且我们甚至没有确凿的证据证明它根本可以解决 LLM 编码助手错误很多的问题。

请注意，我在这里从未谈论修复缺陷的成本。 我只是谈论专业开发人员在专业环境中审查代码以标记问题的能力。 这甚至还没有涉及 LLM 编码助理带来的缺陷的代价有多大。 这是关于审查生成的代码的成本有多大。 无论缺陷是什么或有多少缺陷。 即使法学硕士编码助理非常优秀，这些成本也将保持不变，生产力也会达到相同的极限。

我的意思是，情况可能会更糟

出色地。 坏消息。 情况确实变得更糟。 使用 LLM 编码助手有一个好处，开发人员经常吹捧它们，并全力以赴将它们用作专业工具的一部分。 他们可以输入和生成职业生涯中编写和处理最痛苦的代码。 这里引用了我写这篇文章时的一篇著名的、引人注目的“抨击怀疑论者”博客文章，谈论了法学硕士编码助理可以产生什么。

好吧，你可以让那些总是随机出错的东西来写它。 然后你就可以只复习它，而不必再写了。 我的意思是可能会出现什么问题。 这并不是说它是最难检查错误的代码，对吗？ 正确的？ 正确的 ！？ 我的意思是，也许他们根本没有解决我们是否真的可以以一种有效的方式审查LLM编码助理输出的问题。

但可以肯定的是，他们并没有将其主要用途吹捧为最难有效审查的代码。 正确的 ？！ 是的，他们是。 这就是他们失去我的地方。 他们不会止于不参与“你确定审查代码可以解决问题吗？”的争论。 或者“但是如果我们进行所有这些审查，它真的会更有成效、更好吗？”。 不，他们走得更深。 他们提供的代码是我们所知的专业领域中最难审查和正确使用的代码，也是法学硕士编码助理的最佳应用程序。

怎样做才能让我平静下来

好吧，我会冷静下来。 当然，没有人参与我的论点，当然我一直在指出问题，当然我对那些使用这些工具让他们的生活更轻松的人大喊狼来了。 一切都很好，继续对云大喊大叫，但怎么有人能说服我呢？ 毕竟，我可能是错的，不是吗？ 我的意思是，如果我一直用科学来支持我的观点，我也应该有一些想法来证明我错了，不是吗？

是的。 首先，我希望看到关于人类审阅者发现 LLM 生成代码中缺陷的能力的实证研究。 速度有多快。 以及我们每天能做多少。 我们已经在审查人类生成的代码方面进行了实证研究，所以如果你想说服我我的论点是错误的，我们将需要一组人类审查法学硕士的数据。 我们已经有了一些实验数据和一些经验数据，但数据集的大小和上下文都受到限制。 更多的繁殖会有所帮助。 但请注意，到目前为止，科学数据表明人类不擅长审查 LLM（或者 LLM 擅长避免检测）。 这与预期相符，LLM 接受过逃避检测的训练。 但也许这只是一个侥幸。

其次，您可以尝试向我们展示对法学硕士生成内容的审查与对人工生成内容的审查有何不同。 也许我们拥有的所有这些数据和经验证据并不适用于此。 也许法学硕士有足够的不同，这是一个性质不同的问题。 在这种情况下，我反对法学硕士编码助理的论点就站不住脚了。 请再次注意，到目前为止，有越来越多的初步证据表明，审查法学硕士生成的内容确实是不同的。 但重点是审查它比人类生成的内容更难，这将使我在这里提出的论点更加有力。 所以要小心你搜索的知识。

但我还是疯了

上述论点并不是我成为人工智能怀疑论者的唯一原因，但这可能是我反对法学硕士编码助理的最大原因。 大多数情况下，我不知道，了解所有有关代码审查的信息，作为专业开发人员，我们如何能够从使用此界面和流程的 LLM 工具中获益。 它违背了我们所知道的关于生成高质量代码的一切。

但真正让我发疯的并不是我们不断地获得供应商一次又一次提供的这些工具和流程，尽管有科学证据。 没有什么让我发疯和生气的是被那些甚至从未参与过我提出的问题或问题的人称为“疯子”。 我还没有看到任何一个人工智能支持者参与这个问题或尝试对此进行实证研究。
